channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Hurricane Oscar makes landfall in the Bahamas and heads toward Cuba
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Hurricane Oscar made landfall early Sunday in the southeastern Bahamas and was heading toward Cuba, an island recently beleaguered by a massive power outage. The National Hurricane Center in Miami said the storm's center arrived on Great Inagua island.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Texas man arrested for allegedly stealing neighbors' dogs, killing them
item.description: Deputies went to the home of Stuart Duncan Hammonds, 24, where they found skeletal remains of several animals. Many had head trauma like they had been shot.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shooting at Mississippi trail ride leaves 3 dead, 8 wounded: sheriff
item.description: Three people were killed and eight others were wounded in a shooting at a trail ride party in Holmes County, Mississippi, following a school's homecoming football win.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 7 dead after ferry dock gangway collapses on Georgia's Sapelo Island
item.description: SAVANNAH, Ga. (AP) — Authorities said at least seven people were killed Saturday when part of a ferry dock collapsed on Georgia's Sapelo Island, where crowds had gathered for a fall celebration by the island's tiny Gullah-Geechee community of Black slave descendants.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3 people killed on same Austin, Texas street within one week
item.description: Three people were killed on Burton Drive in southeast Austin within one week, according to the Austin Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Brianna Hollis, NewsNation

item.title: Louisiana urban farmer created sustainable, zero-waste homestead
item.description: Heart and Harvest is impressive, considering its proximity to a burgeoning urban area. With the help of family, the Hoffs use a system of no-till, zero-waste farming techniques to encourage sustainability and combat climate change.
item.dc:creator: Marlo Lacen, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane Oscar forms in the Caribbean
item.description: The formation of Oscar and Nadine comes as parts of the U.S. have been hit hard by a pair of hurricanes, Helene and Milton, that devastated communities, destroyed roads, caused power outages, took lives and injured many.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Haddonfield, Illinois: How ‘Halloween’s’ iconic small town of horror came to be
item.description: Haddonfield, Illinois, a fictional small town, is the setting for John Carpenter's iconic horror film "Halloween," which has become a cultural phenomenon and has inspired numerous sequels and remakes.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, KRON4

item.title: Mail carriers reach tentative contract with USPS that includes pay raises and air-conditioned trucks
item.description: Some 200,000 mail carriers have reached a tentative contract deal with the U.S. Postal Service that includes backdated pay raises and a promise to provide workers with air-conditioned trucks.
item.dc:creator: DAVID SHARP and NICK PERRY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Why experts say keeping standard time is 'undeniably' better for us
item.description: Surveys have found that most Americans want to do away with the twice-a-year changing of the clocks. But whether we should observe daylight saving or standard time year round will depend on who you ask.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Singer Zayn postpones US tour after the death of his former bandmate Liam Payne
item.description: English singer Zayn Malik on Saturday postponed a planned tour to the U.S. following the death of his former One Direction bandmate Liam Payne.
item.dc:creator: NICK PERRY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Child labor violations on the rise, problem could get worse: report
item.description: Between 2011 and 2020, the rate of injuries among workers under 18 nearly doubled, a recent report says.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Kyle Larson opens the third round of NASCAR's playoffs as the title favorite
item.description: Kyle Larson has been lucky of late in Las Vegas, where he opens the third round of NASCAR's playoffs on top of the Cup Series standings and the favorite to win Sunday's race.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Utah court allows Australian student to play high school football after lawsuit filed
item.description: An international student who filed a discrimination lawsuit earlier this month has been allowed to play on his high school's football team.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, KRON4

item.title: NYC officials envision turning Fifth Avenue into a grand boulevard
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Manhattan’s famed luxury store row Fifth Avenue is in line for a major makeover. New York City officials unveiled a plan this week to transform a central portion of the thoroughfare between Bryant Park and Central Park into a more pedestrian-centered boulevard.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lynx force Game 5 of WNBA Finals on Carleton's free throws to beat Liberty 82-80
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — With their season on the line, the Minnesota Lynx did just enough to stave off elimination and force a decisive Game 5 of the WNBA Finals. Bridget Carleton made two free throws with 2 seconds left, and the Lynx beat the New York Liberty 82-80 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Black male teachers are a rarity in preschools. This pioneering program wants to change that
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Before 19-year-old Davontez Johnson found himself in a preschool classroom at Dorothy I. Heights Elementary, he was a senior at a nearby high school who, like many students his age, was unsure of what he wanted to do with his life.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chumash people in California to co-steward marine sanctuary in historic partnership
item.description: For more than 10,000 years, Native Americans have been living along California’s central coast, an area of breathtaking beauty with stunning turquoise waters rich in biodiversity.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Crews halt fast-moving brush fire that burned 2 Oakland homes, forced 500 evacuations
item.description: A fast-moving fire fed by strong winds burned two homes Friday and damaged several others in a hillside neighborhood in the city of Oakland, where hundreds of people were ordered to evacuate before more than 100 firefighters stopped the progress of the flames, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Alonso and Mets extend season again, beating Dodgers 12-6 to send NLCS back to LA for Game 6
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Not easy to knock out these New York Mets. Pete Alonso golfed an early three-run homer and the Mets hammered an ineffective Jack Flaherty, extending the National League Championship Series with a 12-6 victory over the Los Angeles Dodgers in Game 5 on Friday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Iowa school shooter ‘likely displayed warning signs’ before January attack, report finds
item.description: The shooter responsible for two deaths and multiple injuries at an Iowa high school earlier this year “likely displayed warning signs” according to a detailed report from state officials released Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: California woman slashed dog's throat, left it alive in trash: Sacramento deputies
item.description: The woman "felt her life would be easier if she only had to care for one dog and cut one of the dog’s throats," say officials.
item.dc:creator: Veronica Catlin, WGN 9

item.title: Trump microphone cuts out for 20 minutes at Detroit rally
item.description: Trump was in the midst of speaking about his tariff policy at the event on Friday, about to call it “the most beautiful word in the dictionary,” before the microphone went down. Someone handed the former president another microphone to try, but it didn’t work either.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KRON4

item.title: Liam Payne’s 1D bandmates, Cheryl, Simon Cowell and more mourn singer
item.description: Friends, collaborators and fellow musicians have expressed shock and sadness over the death of former One Direction singer Liam Payne, who died in Buenos Aires, Argentina, on Wednesday at age 31.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Texas troopers find 134 illegal immigrants near border, some from Iran, authorities say
item.description: Texas authorities on Thursday caught a large group of illegal immigrants near the southern border as the state steps up efforts to combat the flow of migrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jury paves the way for 2 Live Crew to retake control of records that changed hip-hop
item.description: Miami's 2 Live Crew helped redraw the legal landscape around what hip-hop could be, pushing the boundaries of free speech and taste with their provocative and sexually explicit recordings that led to landmark court decisions protecting the rights of artists.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mexican cartel leader 'El Mayo' Zambada makes court appearance in his US drug trafficking case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ismael “El Mayo” Zambada, a longtime leader of Mexico’s Sinaloa cartel who is facing drug trafficking charges in a U.S. court, appeared for the first time Friday before the judge who is presiding over his case.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump compares imprisoned Jan. 6 rioters to Japanese Americans during WWII
item.description: Nearly 900 people have pleaded guilty to a variety of federal charges in relation to the events of Jan. 6.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Oregon elections office shuts down phone lines amid false info surrounding Trump, voters' pamphlet
item.description: The Oregon Elections Division is shutting down its phone lines after the system was "overwhelmed" by out-of-state callers responding to false information about why Donald Trump is not in the Oregon Voters' Pamphlet.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kustura, Fox 5

item.title: Grandma who texted stranger for Thanksgiving announces breast cancer diagnosis
item.description: The Arizona grandmother who sent a text message to what she believed was her grandson’s phone number, inviting him over for Thanksgiving dinner nearly nine years ago, has announced her diagnosis of breast cancer.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, WGN 9

item.title: Bret Baier says he made 'a mistake' with Trump clip during Harris interview on Fox News
item.description: Fox News anchor Bret Baier said Thursday that he made "a mistake” in not including a specific clip of former President Trump during his interview with Vice President Harris on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Abortion is on the ballot in 10 states. Here's where the measures stand
item.description: Voters in 10 states will decide this November whether to adopt constitutional amendments that could shape the future of abortion access in their states.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, Fox 5

item.title: Daughter claims mother's body 'misplaced' in Memphis cemetery
item.description: "They have no idea where or how she was lost, but they do not know where she is," says Georleshia Minter.
item.dc:creator: Quametra Wilborn, ABC 4

item.title: Texas Supreme Court halts execution of man in shaken baby case after lawmakers' last-minute appeal
item.description: HUNTSVILLE, Texas (AP) — The Texas Supreme Court halted Thursday night’s scheduled execution of a man who would have become the first person in the U.S. put to death for a murder conviction tied to a diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO and MICHAEL GRACZYK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Google Flights adds 'Cheapest' option for travelers who just don't care how they get there
item.description: Google Flights has a new option for immediately finding the “cheapest” flight itinerary, but it may come at a slightly different cost.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4
